skill.name: gemini-search
skill.description: Runs web research through the Gemini CLI and returns sourced Markdown answers for current information requests.
skill.summary: Use Gemini CLI web search to gather current sources and summarize results.
skill.author: AKABANAKK

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs users to run a shell script that invokes the Gemini CLI. The weak cryptography and entropy findings appear to be false positives from Japanese text and shell examples. Publication should wait for human review because the script runs Gemini with --yolo and sends untrusted queries into an external web-search workflow.

high_findings.title: Unrestricted Gemini CLI Execution Mode
high_findings.description: The script invokes gemini with the --yolo flag while passing a user-controlled research request. This can reduce confirmation barriers for an external agent workflow and makes prompt-injection or tool misuse impact higher.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command line clearly includes --yolo and receives user-provided search text. The exact danger depends on the installed Gemini CLI configuration, so confidence is high but not absolute.

medium_findings.title: External Command and Network Search Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run a local shell script that calls the Gemini CLI for web search. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it depends on local executable trust and external network behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script and examples directly show bash execution of scripts/web-search.sh and a gemini command invocation. The behavior is intentional, but it remains a real external-command risk.

medium_findings.title: Prompt Injection Exposure From Web Research
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks Gemini to perform web search and summarize results, but the skill does not include instructions to treat web content as untrusted. Malicious pages could influence the downstream answer.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The web-search design is clear, and no prompt-injection hardening is present in the script. I did not find evidence that the skill itself contains a prompt-injection payload.

low_findings.title: Static Entropy and Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-entropy and weak-cryptography alerts point to Japanese prose, Markdown examples, and a shell script. I found no cryptographic implementation, encoded payload, or encrypted binary content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable Markdown and shell text, not obfuscated data or cryptographic code. The scanner likely misclassified non-English text and command examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: gemini --yolo
dangerous_patterns.description: The script calls gemini with --yolo while embedding the user search request in the prompt. This pattern should be removed or constrained before marketplace publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous flag is directly visible in the command invocation. Risk depends on Gemini CLI permissions, but the pattern is concrete.

content.user_title: Search the web with Gemini CLI
content.value_statement: Current research often needs fresh sources beyond the model context window. This skill sends a research prompt to Gemini CLI and asks for sourced Markdown answers.
actual_capabilities: Runs a shell script that calls the Gemini CLI.
Accepts a natural-language search question as one argument.
Requests detailed Markdown answers from Gemini.
Asks Gemini to include all referenced URLs.
Provides examples for technical research, news, errors, and comparisons.
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI to be installed and configured locally.
Uses an external network service through Gemini.
Does not verify source quality inside the shell script.
Uses --yolo, which needs security review before publication.

use_cases.title: Research framework updates
use_cases.description: Find current release notes and technical articles for web frameworks.

use_cases.title: Investigate error messages
use_cases.description: Search for related documentation, issues, and discussions for a specific error.

use_cases.title: Compare technical choices
use_cases.description: Gather sources that compare libraries, tools, or implementation approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Basic current search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find recent information about one topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for current information about [topic]. Summarize the main points and include the source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Official documentation search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search official documentation for [technology] about [feature]. Explain the correct usage and include source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Error investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for causes and fixes for this error: [error text]. Prefer official docs and well-documented issue threads.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative research brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [option A] and [option B] for [use case]. Include tradeoffs, recommendations, and URLs for each claim.

output_examples.input: Search for the latest Next.js release changes.
output_examples.output: A Markdown summary of release highlights, migration notes, and links to official release notes.

output_examples.input: Find causes of a TypeScript assignment error.
output_examples.output: A concise explanation of common causes, practical fixes, and links to relevant documentation or issue discussions.

output_examples.input: Compare Vite and Webpack for a new project.
output_examples.output: A sourced comparison covering build speed, ecosystem fit, configuration complexity, and recommended use cases.

best_practices: Ask for official sources when accuracy matters.
Review all returned URLs before relying on conclusions.
Run the script only in an environment where Gemini CLI permissions are understood.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for secrets or private data.
Do not treat web results as verified without review.
Do not publish with --yolo enabled without a security exception.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It runs a Gemini CLI web-search prompt and asks for a sourced Markdown answer.

faq.question: Does it require a local dependency?
faq.answer: Yes. The gemini command must be installed, authenticated, and available in the shell path.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for web search through Gemini CLI.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as submitted?
faq.answer: No. The audit recommends review because the script uses gemini with --yolo.

faq.question: Can it search official documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show prompts that request official documentation and release notes.

faq.question: What should users verify?
faq.answer: Users should verify returned URLs, source quality, and Gemini CLI permissions before trusting results.
